title: Class members should not be initialized with dangling references
description: Class members should not be initialized with dangling referencesAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugpitfallWhy is this an issue?Special attention should be paid when initializing class members: it is easy to get it wrong and initialize them with references that are going to be invalidated at the end of the constructor, known as dangling references.Noncompliant code examplestruct S { int *x; int &y; S(int i, int j) : x(&i), // Noncompliant, initializing x to the stack address of i y(j) // Noncompliant, y is bound to variable j which has a shorter lifetime {} };Compliant solutionstruct S { int *x; int &y; S(int *i, int &j) : x(i), y(j) {} };Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositorie
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.
